many: A university’s mathematics department has many professors, specifically 10, and will offer many courses, specifically 20, next semester. Each of these 10 professors will be assigned exactly 2 of the 20 courses, and each course will be taught by exactly one professor. If any professor may be assigned to teach any course, in how many different ways can the department assign professors to all 20 courses so that every professor teaches exactly 2 courses and every course has exactly one instructor?

A university has many mathematics professors — precisely 10 — and will run many different courses next semester — precisely 20. Each professor is to be responsible for exactly 2 distinct courses, and every course must be taught by exactly one professor. If any of the professors may be assigned to teach any of the courses, in how many distinct complete assignments of the 10 professors to the 20 courses are possible?

A college has many mathematics faculty members: there are 10 professors available to teach many different classes, with 20 distinct course sections offered next term. Each professor will be responsible for exactly 2 different course sections, and every section must be taught by exactly one professor. adjusted: A university mathematics department must adjust its teaching schedule: there are 10 professors and 20 distinct courses next semester, and after the adjustment each professor will be assigned exactly two different courses while every course is taught by exactly one professor; if any professor may teach any course, in how many distinct ways can the department assign professors to courses?

An adjusted scheduling problem: a university math department with 10 professors must assign each professor to teach exactly 2 of the 20 distinct courses offered next semester, with every course taught by exactly one professor; assuming any professor may be assigned to any course and assignments are distinguished only by which professor teaches which course, in how many different complete assignments of the 10 professors to the 20 courses are possible?

A university math department has 10 professors and needs to assign them so that each professor teaches exactly two of the 20 distinct courses offered next semester, with each course taught by exactly one professor; assuming any professor can be assigned to any course and treating assignments that differ only by swapping the two courses taught by a given professor as adjusted into the same labeling, how many complete assignments of professors to courses are possible?
focused: A math department has 10 professors and 20 distinct courses. Each professor is to be assigned exactly two different courses, and each course must be taught by exactly one professor. improbable: A college math department with 10 professors must staff 20 distinct courses next term so that every course has exactly one instructor and each professor teaches exactly two courses; assuming it is not forbidden or otherwise improbable that any professor could be assigned to any course, in how many distinct complete assignment patterns can the department allocate professors to the 20 courses?

It seems improbable that no constraints prevent any professor from teaching any course: the mathematics department has 10 professors who together will cover 20 distinct courses next term, with each professor assigned exactly 2 courses and each course taught by exactly one professor; under these conditions, in how many distinct ways can the 20 courses be assigned to the 10 professors?

blended: A university math department with 10 professors must staff 20 distinct courses next semester, assigning each professor to teach exactly two courses and each course to a single professor; if any professor may be paired with any course, in how many blended ways can the full assignment of professors to the 20 courses be arranged?

overriding: A mathematics department needs to assign each of its 10 professors to teach exactly 2 of the 20 distinct courses offered next semester so that every course has exactly one professor; treating professors as distinct and courses as distinct, in how many ways can the department make a complete assignment if any professor may be assigned to any course, with no additional restrictions or overrides on assignments?

A department must override default scheduling so that each of 10 faculty members is assigned exactly two of the 20 distinct courses next term, with every course taught by exactly one faculty member; if any faculty member may be assigned to any course, in how many different complete assignment configurations (counting which professor teaches which two courses) can the department override the schedule to produce a full assignment?

A mathematics department has 10 faculty members and there are 20 distinct courses to be taught next term. How many complete assignments of faculty to courses are possible, treating assignments that differ only by swapping the order of the two courses assigned to a given faculty member as identical?
bipartite: Consider a bipartite assignment between a set of 10 professors and a set of 20 distinct courses where each professor is matched to exactly two courses and each course is matched to exactly one professor; if every professor is eligible to teach any course, in how many distinct complete bipartite matchings (assignments of professors to courses) are possible?

In a bipartite assignment problem, one part consists of 10 professors and the other consists of 20 distinct courses; each professor is to be matched to exactly two courses and each course to exactly one professor, with every professor eligible to teach any course—how many distinct complete matchings of professors to courses are there?
